title: dregea stelostigma
intro: Dregea stelostigma, a plant species belonging to the Asclepiadaceae family, is commonly known as the "Indian root" or "wild jasmine". Traditionally, the roots and tubers of Dregea stelostigma have been used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues like asthma and bronchitis. Dregea stelostigma is often prepared as a herbal decoction or tincture for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
